findings.message: TASK-02's telemetry plan is not implementable within the stated scope. `appendTelemetryEvent` only accepts `email_draft_created | ...` in `packages/mcp-server/src/tools/gmail.ts`, and `packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/booking-email.test.ts` asserts that same event. Changing booking-email telemetry to `email_sent` also requires shared telemetry type/schema/test updates that are not listed in the task's affected files or rollout.

TASK-02 understates the blast radius. `apps/reception/src/components/checkins/EmailBookingButton.tsx` still hardcodes "Create booking email draft", "Email draft created", and "Failed to create email draft", so updating only `booking-email.ts` and `useBookingEmail.ts` will leave the live UI still describing draft creation instead of sent email.

The plan promises staff can see who last actioned each reminder "and when", but TASK-03 only implements `lastActionedBy`. The task acceptance and implementation details never add or render a `lastActionedAt` field, and existing `hoursElapsed` is only a relative timer, not the actual action timestamp.

TASK-02 quietly changes label-application failure from a hard failure to a logged success, but the current MCP contract and tests treat failed `applyDraftOutcomeLabelsStrict()` as an error. If that behavior is being relaxed, the plan needs to call out the contract change explicitly and include the corresponding test updates, not only mention it as edge-case handling.
